touches.ai_summary: Initial outreach via LinkedIn to Lauren Anderson. Introduced AskElephant's AI-powered conversation intelligence platform and its value for CS team productivity. Lauren responded positively, expressing interest in learning more about how we differ from their current evaluation of Chorus.

questions_asked.question: How does AskElephant compare to Chorus for CS teams specifically?
questions_asked.answer: AskElephant is built with post-sale teams in mind - our automation workflows are designed for CS handoffs, renewal signals, and health scoring rather than just sales pipeline. Happy to show you a demo.

next_steps: Lauren agreed to a 15-minute qualification call next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Lauren Anderson, Director of CS. Discussed current state: 9-person CS team struggling with manual note-taking and inconsistent CRM hygiene. Currently evaluating Chorus but concerned about sales-first feature set. Lauren confirmed budget authority for tools under $10k and timeline to decide by end of quarter. She's the primary decision-maker for CS tooling but mentioned their VP of Revenue would need to sign off on any new vendor.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We just lost two key accounts because early warning signs were buried in call notes no one read. Leadership wants better visibility into customer health.

questions_asked.question: Can AskElephant integrate with our existing Salesforce setup?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration with bi-directional sync for accounts, contacts, and custom activity logging.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Just me for now - I own CS tools. Our VP of Revenue, Mike, will need to approve the contract but he usually defers to my recommendation on CS-specific platforms.

next_steps: Schedule full demo for Lauren and her team lead

touches.ai_summary: Demo call with Lauren Anderson and one CS team lead. Walked through core features: call recording, AI summarization, automated CRM logging, and custom playbook automations for CS workflows. Team engaged well with the automation builder and asked detailed questions about churn signal detection. Lauren compared several features to Chorus demo she saw last week - expressed preference for our CS-focused analytics but noted Chorus has stronger executive dashboard reporting. Discussed pricing: $8,300 for 9 seats on automations_plus tier with onboarding/consulting included. Lauren seemed comfortable with price point but hesitant to move forward without getting Mike (VP Revenue) involved at some point.

questions_asked.question: How does the sentiment analysis work for customer health scoring?
questions_asked.answer: We analyze tone, keyword patterns, and engagement metrics across all customer touchpoints, then surface risk scores in Salesforce. You can customize the weight of different signals.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom automations for our specific renewal workflow?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - that's included in the automations_plus tier. Our team will help you configure playbooks during onboarding based on your process.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer decision for you?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, I love the product. I just need to figure out the right time to loop in Mike. He's slammed with board prep right now.

questions_asked.question: What's your typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are live within 2 weeks - 1 week for technical setup and integrations, 1 week for team onboarding and playbook configuration.

objections: Chorus has more robust executive-level reporting dashboards that leadership might prefer
next_steps: Lauren to find time to brief Mike on the evaluation

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent to Lauren with proposal summary, pricing breakdown for 9 seats at $8,300 ACV, and ROI calculator showing projected time savings for her CS team. Also included case study from similar professional services company. Reminded her of end-of-quarter timing and offered to join a brief call with Mike if helpful to accelerate decision. Lauren responded within a few hours saying she's still working on getting time with Mike but remains interested - asked if we could hold pricing through end of month while she navigates internal scheduling.

questions_asked.question: Can you hold this pricing if we don't sign until late April?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, I can honor this quote through end of April. Let me know if there's anything I can do to help get Mike's input sooner.

next_steps: Lauren to schedule time with VP of Revenue
Check back in one week if no update
